tactics.name: Initial Access
tactics.description: The adversary is trying to get into your network.

techniques.name: Phishing
techniques.description: Adversaries may send phishing messages to gain access to victim systems.

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to exploit a weakness in an Internet-facing host or system to gain access.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of valid accounts to access systems.

techniques.name: Impair Defenses
techniques.description: Adversaries may maliciously modify components of a victim environment to weaken or disable security defenses.

techniques.name: External Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries may use external remote services to initially access victim systems.

tactics.name: Execution
tactics.description: The adversary is trying to run malicious code.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse command and scripting interpreters to execute commands, scripts, or binaries.

techniques.name: Scheduled Task/Job
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse task scheduling functionality to facilitate initial or recurring execution of malicious code.

techniques.name: User Execution
techniques.description: An adversary may rely upon specific actions by a user in order to gain execution.

techniques.name: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
techniques.description: Adversaries may achieve persistence by adding a program to a startup folder or referencing it with a registry run key.

techniques.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
techniques.description: Adversaries may configure system settings to automatically execute a program during system boot or logon.

techniques.name: Trusted Developer Utilities Proxy Execution
techniques.description: Adversaries may use trusted developer utilities to proxy execution of malicious code.

tactics.name: Persistence
tactics.description: The adversary is trying to maintain their foothold.

techniques.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
techniques.description: Adversaries may configure system settings to automatically execute a program during system boot or logon.

techniques.name: Create Account
techniques.description: Adversaries may create an account to maintain access to victim systems.

techniques.name: Account Manipulation
techniques.description: Adversaries may manipulate accounts to maintain access to victim systems.

techniques.name: Event Triggered Execution
techniques.description: Adversaries may establish persistence by executing malicious content triggered by an event.

techniques.name: Bootkit
techniques.description: Adversaries may use bootkits to persist on systems at a level below the operating system.

tactics.name: Privilege Escalation
tactics.description: The adversary is trying to gain higher-level permissions.

techniques.name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism
techniques.description: Adversaries may circumvent mechanisms designed to control elevation of user privileges.

techniques.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
techniques.description: Adversaries may exploit software vulnerabilities to gain elevated privileges.

techniques.name: Domain Policy Modification
techniques.description: Adversaries may modify Active Directory domain policies to gain elevated privileges.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of valid accounts to access systems.

techniques.name: Create or Modify System Process
techniques.description: Adversaries may create or modify system processes to execute malicious code with elevated privileges.

tactics.name: Defense Evasion
tactics.description: The adversary is trying to avoid being detected.

techniques.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to make an executable or file difficult to discover or analyze.

techniques.name: Impair Defenses
techniques.description: Adversaries may maliciously modify components of a victim environment to weaken or disable security defenses.

techniques.name: Indicator Removal
techniques.description: Adversaries may delete or modify artifacts left on a system after an intrusion.

techniques.name: Disable or Modify Tools
techniques.description: Adversaries may disable or modify security tools to avoid detection.

techniques.name: Data from Local System
techniques.description: Adversaries may search local file systems and remote file shares for sensitive information.

techniques.name: Deobfuscate/Decode Files or Information
techniques.description: Adversaries may deobfuscate or decode files or information that were previously obfuscated or encoded.

techniques.name: Masquerading
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to manipulate features of their artifacts to make them appear legitimate or benign.

tactics.name: Credential Access
tactics.description: The adversary is trying to steal account names and passwords.

techniques.name: OS Credential Dumping
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to extract credential material from the operating system and software.

techniques.name: Credentials from Password Stores
techniques.description: Adversaries may search for common password storage locations to obtain user credentials.

techniques.name: Input Capture
techniques.description: Adversaries may use methods to capture user input, such as keystroke logging.

techniques.name: Unsecured Credentials
techniques.description: Adversaries may search for insecurely stored credentials in various locations on a system.

techniques.name: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to steal or forge Kerberos tickets to gain access to systems.

techniques.name: Brute Force
techniques.description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to attempt to gain access to accounts.

tactics.name: Discovery
tactics.description: The adversary is trying to figure out your environment.

techniques.name: Account Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of local system or domain accounts.

techniques.name: System Information Discovery
techniques.description: An adversary may attempt to get detailed information about the operating system and hardware.

techniques.name: File and Directory Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may enumerate files and directories or search for specific information.

techniques.name: System Network Configuration Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may gather information about the network configuration and topology.

techniques.name: Remote System Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to discover remote systems on a network.

techniques.name: Permission Groups Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to find local or domain groups and permission settings.

tactics.name: Lateral Movement
tactics.description: The adversary is trying to move through your environment.

techniques.name: Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries may use Valid Accounts to log into a service that accepts remote connections.

techniques.name: Pass the Hash
techniques.description: Adversaries may pass the hash to authenticate to a remote system.

techniques.name: Use Alternate Authentication Material
techniques.description: Adversaries may use alternate authentication materials to bypass normal authentication.

techniques.name: Automated Collection
techniques.description: Adversaries may use automated techniques to collect data from a network.

techniques.name: Windows Management Instrumentation
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse Windows Management Instrumentation to move laterally.

tactics.name: Collection
tactics.description: The adversary is trying to gather data of interest.

techniques.name: Data from Local System
techniques.description: Adversaries may search local file systems and remote file shares for sensitive information.

techniques.name: Screen Capture
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to take screen captures of the desktop to gather information.

techniques.name: Email Collection
techniques.description: Adversaries may target user email to collect sensitive information.

techniques.name: Clipboard Data
techniques.description: Adversaries may collect data from the clipboard.

techniques.name: Data Staged
techniques.description: Adversaries may stage collected data in a central location before exfiltration.

tactics.name: Command and Control
tactics.description: The adversary is trying to communicate with compromised systems.

techniques.name: Application Layer Protocol
techniques.description: Adversaries may use common application layer protocols to blend with normal network traffic.

techniques.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
techniques.description: Adversaries may transfer tools or files to a compromised system.

techniques.name: Non-Standard Port
techniques.description: Adversaries may use non-standard ports for command and control communications.

techniques.name: Software Deployment Tools
techniques.description: Adversaries may use legitimate software deployment tools to establish C2 channels.

tactics.name: Exfiltration
tactics.description: The adversary is trying to steal your data.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Command and Control Channel
techniques.description: Adversaries may steal data by exfiltrating it over an existing command and control channel.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
techniques.description: Adversaries may steal data by exfiltrating it over a different protocol than the C2 channel.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
techniques.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data over web services such as cloud storage.

techniques.name: Automated Exfiltration
techniques.description: Adversaries may use automated techniques to exfiltrate data.

tactics.name: Impact
tactics.description: The adversary is trying to manipulate, interrupt, or destroy your systems and data.

techniques.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
techniques.description: Adversaries may encrypt data on target systems to disrupt availability.

techniques.name: Inhibit System Recovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may delete or remove built-in operating system data and utilities.

techniques.name: Data Destruction
techniques.description: Adversaries may destroy data and files on specific systems.

techniques.name: Service Stop
techniques.description: Adversaries may stop services to disrupt system functionality.

techniques.name: Data Manipulation
techniques.description: Adversaries may manipulate data to affect system integrity or cause confusion.
